# Server-Side Rendering [Ship pre-positioned items in the server HTML]

Rendering happens client-side by default: the server emits an empty grid and items appear once the browser has measured them. Passing the `ssr` option instead lays the grid out on the server, so the first paint already carries positioned items.

`estimateSize` becomes **required** — the server has no DOM to measure, so it is the only height source. Without it every item collapses to `0`.

:::warning
SSR is window-scoped only. `ssr` and `scrollElementRef` are mutually exclusive — passing both is a compile error. See [Scrolling in a Container](/docs/scrolling-in-a-container).
:::

## With the component

Pass `ssr` alongside `estimateSize`:

```tsx twoslash
// @twoslash-cache: {"v":1,"hash":"66a33a259cd468207bb63f5eb560f381875de2f5eb5974ebcf5006ab38ddacf2","data":""}
import { Masonry } from 'kaskaid';

const HEIGHTS = [200, 600, 200, 400, 100, 100, 50, 200];

<Masonry
  data={HEIGHTS}
  ssr={{ itemCount: HEIGHTS.length, lanes: 4 }}
  estimateSize={(i) => HEIGHTS[i]}
  renderItem={({ item, index }) => <div style={{ height: item }}>{index}</div>}
/>;
```

## With the hook

`ssr` is a plain [`useMasonry`](/docs/api/use-masonry) option, so a custom element structure gets the same server-rendered layout. Before mount, `items` holds the first `ssr.itemCount` entries from the measurements cache; after mount it becomes the real visible window.

```tsx twoslash
// @twoslash-cache: {"v":1,"hash":"f0abdd11eeaf9cc13655d853fdeee9a02a558f51437e399ae5522ba03ae4efb1","data":""}
import { useMasonry } from 'kaskaid';

const HEIGHTS = [200, 600, 200, 400, 100, 100, 50, 200];

function Grid() {
  const { gridProps, getItemProps, items } = useMasonry({
    data: HEIGHTS,
    ssr: { itemCount: HEIGHTS.length, lanes: 4 },
    estimateSize: (i) => HEIGHTS[i],
  });

  return (
    <section {...gridProps}>
      {items.map((item) => (
        <article key={item.key} {...getItemProps(item)}>
          <div style={{ height: HEIGHTS[item.index] }}>{item.index}</div>
        </article>
      ))}
    </section>
  );
}
```

## Cost

:::warning
Server-side layout cost scales with `data.length`, not `itemCount` — lane assignment is order-dependent, so the whole list is laid out per request.
:::

`lanes` (default `4`) is the lane count used for the server layout and the first paint, before CSS resolves `--lanes` on the client. Pick the value your most common viewport lands on; see [Styling & Responsive Lanes](/docs/styling).

## Reference

- [`<Masonry />` › ssr](/docs/api/masonry#ssr) — the full `SSRConfig` field table
- [`useMasonry` › ssr](/docs/api/use-masonry#ssr)
